page: OVHcloud h100-380 is a GPU-accelerated server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 9354 processor with 30 dedicated physical cores running at 3.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, with one thread per core. The hardware configuration includes 380.0 GB of system memory, providing 12.67 GB of RAM per core. For acceleration, it integrates a single NVIDIA Hopper H100 GPU with 80 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage consists of a 4040 GB NVMe SSD, and the network interface delivers an 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates top-tier performance in single-core CPU execution and large language model inference tasks. Specifically, LLM prompt processing and text generation speeds for 135M, 7B, and 70B models rank in the top 10% of tested systems. Multi-core CPU performance, database operations, and Redis SET operations are strong, placing in the top 25%. However, a notable hardware tradeoff is observed in memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks, which registers in the bottom 25% for both read and write operations, contrasting with the top 25% performance seen in small-block cached memory reads.

Given the inclusion of a high-end NVIDIA Hopper GPU and a substantial NVMe storage array, this server offers high resource density for specialized workloads. It is highly suited for deep learning, large language model inference, and data-intensive database operations. The dedicated AMD EPYC cores also make it appropriate for parallelized compilation and cryptographic tasks, though the uncached memory bandwidth limitation should be considered for workloads requiring continuous large-block memory transfers.
description: OVHcloud h100-380 is a GPU-accelerated server running on the KVM hypervisor, featuring an AMD EPYC 9354 processor with 30 dedicated physical cores on the x86_64 architecture, operating at 3.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 380.0 GB of system memory, a 4040 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA Hopper H100 GPU with 80 GB of VRAM. It delivers top-tier performance in LLM inference tasks and single-core CPU benchmarks, alongside strong multi-core and database execution. A performance tradeoff exists in large-block uncached memory bandwidth, which ranks in the bottom 25%, contrasting with strong small-block cached memory reads. This server is designed for deep learning, large language model inference, and high-performance database workloads.

meta_description: OVHcloud h100-380 is a GPU-accelerated server with 30 AMD EPYC cores, 380 GB RAM, a 4040 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA Hopper H100 GPU.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated server combining dedicated AMD EPYC processors, massive NVMe storage, and an NVIDIA H100 GPU for demanding AI workloads.
bullet_points: NVIDIA Hopper H100 GPU with 80 GB VRAM for accelerated AI and deep learning workloads.
30 dedicated AMD EPYC 9354 physical cores running at 3.0 GHz.
380 GB of system memory and 4040 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
Top-tier LLM inference performance across small, medium, and large models.
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and KVM hypervisor virtualization.
